types.name: IncludeEnum
types.description:
Specify additional output data to include in the model response. Currently supported values are:
- `web_search_call.results`: Include the search results of the web search tool call.
- `web_search_call.action.sources`: Include the sources of the web search tool call.
- `code_interpreter_call.outputs`: Includes the outputs of python code execution in code interpreter tool call items.
- `computer_call_output.output.image_url`: Include image urls from the computer call output.
- `file_search_call.results`: Include the search results of the file search tool call.
- `message.input_image.image_url`: Include image urls from the input message.
- `message.output_text.logprobs`: Include logprobs with assistant messages.
- `reasoning.encrypted_content`: Includes an encrypted version of reasoning tokens in reasoning item outputs. This enables reasoning items to be used in multi-turn conversations when using the Responses API statelessly (like when the `store` parameter is set to `false`, or when an organization is enrolled in the zero data retention program).

fields.name: user
fields.description:
This field is being replaced by `safety_identifier` and `prompt_cache_key`. Use `prompt_cache_key` instead to maintain caching optimizations.
A stable identifier for your end-users.
Used to boost cache hit rates by better bucketing similar requests and  to help OpenAI detect and prevent abuse. [Learn more](/docs/guides/safety-best-practices#safety-identifiers).

fields.name: safety_identifier
fields.description:
A stable identifier used to help detect users of your application that may be violating OpenAI's usage policies.
The IDs should be a string that uniquely identifies each user, with a maximum length of 64 characters. We recommend hashing their username or email address, in order to avoid sending us any identifying information. [Learn more](/docs/guides/safety-best-practices#safety-identifiers).

fields.name: prompt_cache_key
fields.description: Used by OpenAI to cache responses for similar requests to optimize your cache hit rates. Replaces the `user` field. [Learn more](/docs/guides/prompt-caching).

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this Response.

fields.name: object
fields.description: The object type of this resource - always set to `response`.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
The status of the response generation. One of `completed`, `failed`,
`in_progress`, `cancelled`, `queued`, or `incomplete`.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when this Response was created.

fields.name: output
fields.description:
An array of content items generated by the model.

- The length and order of items in the `output` array is dependent
  on the model's response.
- Rather than accessing the first item in the `output` array and
  assuming it's an `assistant` message with the content generated by
  the model, you might consider using the `output_text` property where
  supported in SDKs.

fields.name: parallel_tool_calls
fields.description: Whether to allow the model to run tool calls in parallel.

types.name: ServiceTier
types.description:
Specifies the processing type used for serving the request.
  - If set to 'auto', then the request will be processed with the service tier configured in the Project settings. Unless otherwise configured, the Project will use 'default'.
  - If set to 'default', then the request will be processed with the standard pricing and performance for the selected model.
  - If set to '[flex](/docs/guides/flex-processing)' or '[priority](https://openai.com/api-priority-processing/)', then the request will be processed with the corresponding service tier.
  - When not set, the default behavior is 'auto'.

  When the `service_tier` parameter is set, the response body will include the `service_tier` value based on the processing mode actually used to serve the request. This response value may be different from the value set in the parameter.

types.name: ResponsePromptCacheRetention
types.description: The retention policy for the prompt cache. Set to `24h` to enable extended prompt caching, which keeps cached prefixes active for longer, up to a maximum of 24 hours. [Learn more](/docs/guides/prompt-caching#prompt-cache-retention).

types.name: Reasoning
types.description:
**gpt-5 and o-series models only**

Configuration options for
[reasoning models](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning).

types.name: ReasoningEffort
types.description:
Constrains effort on reasoning for
[reasoning models](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning).
Currently supported values are `none`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, and `xhigh`. Reducing
reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used
on reasoning in a response.

- `gpt-5.1` defaults to `none`, which does not perform reasoning. The supported reasoning values for `gpt-5.1` are `none`, `low`, `medium`, and `high`. Tool calls are supported for all reasoning values in gpt-5.1.
- All models before `gpt-5.1` default to `medium` reasoning effort, and do not support `none`.
- The `gpt-5-pro` model defaults to (and only supports) `high` reasoning effort.
- `xhigh` is supported for all models after `gpt-5.1-codex-max`.

types.name: ReasoningSummary
types.description:
A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be
useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process.
One of `auto`, `concise`, or `detailed`.

`concise` is supported for `computer-use-preview` models and all reasoning models after `gpt-5`.

types.name: ResponseTextParam
types.description:
Configuration options for a text response from the model. Can be plain
text or structured JSON data. Learn more:
- [Text inputs and outputs](/docs/guides/text)
- [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs)

types.name: TextResponseFormatConfiguration
types.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Configuring `{ "type": "json_schema" }` enables Structured Outputs, 
which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON schema. Learn more in the 
[Structured Outputs guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

The default format is `{ "type": "text" }` with no additional options.

**Not recommended for gpt-4o and newer models:**

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables the older JSON mode, which
ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using `json_schema`
is preferred for models that support it.

types.name: ResponseFormatText

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `text`.

types.description: Default response format. Used to generate text responses.

types.name: TextResponseFormatJsonSchema

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to
determine how to respond in the format.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain
underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

types.description:
JSON Schema response format. Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

types.name: ResponseFormatJsonObject

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_object`.

types.description:
JSON object response format. An older method of generating JSON responses.
Using `json_schema` is recommended for models that support it. Note that the
model will not generate JSON without a system or user message instructing it
to do so.

types.name: Verbosity
types.description:
Constrains the verbosity of the model's response. Lower values will result in
more concise responses, while higher values will result in more verbose responses.
Currently supported values are `low`, `medium`, and `high`.

types.name: Tool
types.description: A tool that can be used to generate a response.

types.name: FunctionTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the function tool. Always `function`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to call.

fields.name: defer_loading
fields.description: Whether this function is deferred and loaded via tool search.

types.description: Defines a function in your own code the model can choose to call. Learn more about [function calling](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling).

types.name: FileSearchTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the file search tool. Always `file_search`.

fields.name: vector_store_ids
fields.description: The IDs of the vector stores to search.

fields.name: max_num_results
fields.description: The maximum number of results to return. This number should be between 1 and 50 inclusive.

types.description: A tool that searches for relevant content from uploaded files. Learn more about the [file search tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-file-search).

fields.name: score_threshold
fields.description: The score threshold for the file search, a number between 0 and 1. Numbers closer to 1 will attempt to return only the most relevant results, but may return fewer results.

fields.name: embedding_weight
fields.description: The weight of the embedding in the reciprocal ranking fusion.

fields.name: text_weight
fields.description: The weight of the text in the reciprocal ranking fusion.

types.name: ComparisonFilter

fields.name: type
fields.description:
Specifies the comparison operator: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `in`, `nin`.
- `eq`: equals
- `ne`: not equal
- `gt`: greater than
- `gte`: greater than or equal
- `lt`: less than
- `lte`: less than or equal
- `in`: in
- `nin`: not in

fields.name: key
fields.description: The key to compare against the value.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The value to compare against the attribute key; supports string, number, or boolean types.

types.description: A filter used to compare a specified attribute key to a given value using a defined comparison operation.

types.name: ComparisonFilterType
types.description:
Specifies the comparison operator: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `in`, `nin`.
- `eq`: equals
- `ne`: not equal
- `gt`: greater than
- `gte`: greater than or equal
- `lt`: less than
- `lte`: less than or equal
- `in`: in
- `nin`: not in

types.name: ComparisonFilterValue
types.description: The value to compare against the attribute key; supports string, number, or boolean types.

types.name: CompoundFilter

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of operation: `and` or `or`.

fields.name: filters
fields.description: Array of filters to combine. Items can be `ComparisonFilter` or `CompoundFilter`.

types.description: Combine multiple filters using `and` or `or`.

types.name: CompoundFilterType
types.description: Type of operation: `and` or `or`.

types.name: ComputerTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the computer tool. Always `computer`.

types.description: A tool that controls a virtual computer. Learn more about the [computer tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-computer-use).

types.name: ComputerUsePreviewTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the computer use tool. Always `computer_use_preview`.

fields.name: display_width
fields.description: The width of the computer display.

fields.name: display_height
fields.description: The height of the computer display.

types.description: A tool that controls a virtual computer. Learn more about the [computer tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-computer-use).

types.name: WebSearchTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the web search tool. One of `web_search` or `web_search_2025_08_26`.

fields.name: search_context_size
fields.description: High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the search. One of `low`, `medium`, or `high`. `medium` is the default.

types.description:
Search the Internet for sources related to the prompt. Learn more about the
[web search tool](/docs/guides/tools-web-search).

types.name: WebSearchToolType
types.description: The type of the web search tool. One of `web_search` or `web_search_2025_08_26`.

types.name: WebSearchToolFilters
types.description: Filters for the search.

types.name: WebSearchApproximateLocation

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of location approximation. Always `approximate`.

types.description: The approximate location of the user.

types.name: WebSearchToolSearchContextSize
types.description: High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the search. One of `low`, `medium`, or `high`. `medium` is the default.

types.name: McpTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the MCP tool. Always `mcp`.

fields.name: server_label
fields.description: A label for this MCP server, used to identify it in tool calls.

fields.name: server_url
fields.description:
The URL for the MCP server. One of `server_url` or `connector_id` must be
provided.

fields.name: connector_id
fields.description:
Identifier for service connectors, like those available in ChatGPT. One of
`server_url` or `connector_id` must be provided. Learn more about service
connectors [here](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp#connectors).

Currently supported `connector_id` values are:

- Dropbox: `connector_dropbox`
- Gmail: `connector_gmail`
- Google Calendar: `connector_googlecalendar`
- Google Drive: `connector_googledrive`
- Microsoft Teams: `connector_microsoftteams`
- Outlook Calendar: `connector_outlookcalendar`
- Outlook Email: `connector_outlookemail`
- SharePoint: `connector_sharepoint`

fields.name: authorization
fields.description:
An OAuth access token that can be used with a remote MCP server, either
with a custom MCP server URL or a service connector. Your application
must handle the OAuth authorization flow and provide the token here.

fields.name: server_description
fields.description: Optional description of the MCP server, used to provide more context.

fields.name: defer_loading
fields.description: Whether this MCP tool is deferred and discovered via tool search.

types.description:
Give the model access to additional tools via remote Model Context Protocol
(MCP) servers. [Learn more about MCP](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp).

types.name: McpToolConnectorId
types.description:
Identifier for service connectors, like those available in ChatGPT. One of
`server_url` or `connector_id` must be provided. Learn more about service
connectors [here](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp#connectors).

Currently supported `connector_id` values are:

- Dropbox: `connector_dropbox`
- Gmail: `connector_gmail`
- Google Calendar: `connector_googlecalendar`
- Google Drive: `connector_googledrive`
- Microsoft Teams: `connector_microsoftteams`
- Outlook Calendar: `connector_outlookcalendar`
- Outlook Email: `connector_outlookemail`
- SharePoint: `connector_sharepoint`

types.name: McpToolAllowedTools
types.description: List of allowed tool names or a filter object.

types.name: McpToolFilter

fields.name: tool_names
fields.description: List of allowed tool names.

fields.name: read_only
fields.description:
Indicates whether or not a tool modifies data or is read-only. If an
MCP server is [annotated with `readOnlyHint`](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/schema#toolannotations-readonlyhint),
it will match this filter.

types.description: A filter object to specify which tools are allowed.

types.name: McpToolRequireApproval
types.description: Specify which of the MCP server's tools require approval.

types.name: McpToolRequireApprovalMcpToolApprovalFilter
types.description:
Specify which of the MCP server's tools require approval. Can be
`always`, `never`, or a filter object associated with tools
that require approval.

types.name: McpToolRequireApprovalMcpToolApprovalSetting
types.description:
Specify a single approval policy for all tools. One of `always` or
`never`. When set to `always`, all tools will require approval. When
set to `never`, all tools will not require approval.

types.name: CodeInterpreterTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the code interpreter tool. Always `code_interpreter`.

fields.name: container
fields.description:
The code interpreter container. Can be a container ID or an object that
specifies uploaded file IDs to make available to your code, along with an
optional `memory_limit` setting.

types.description: A tool that runs Python code to help generate a response to a prompt.

types.name: CodeInterpreterToolContainer
types.description:
The code interpreter container. Can be a container ID or an object that
specifies uploaded file IDs to make available to your code, along with an
optional `memory_limit` setting.

types.name: AutoCodeInterpreterToolParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Always `auto`.

fields.name: file_ids
fields.description: An optional list of uploaded files to make available to your code.

fields.name: network_policy
fields.description: Network access policy for the container.

types.description: Configuration for a code interpreter container. Optionally specify the IDs of the files to run the code on.

types.name: AutoCodeInterpreterToolParamNetworkPolicy
types.description: Network access policy for the container.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Disable outbound network access. Always `disabled`.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Allow outbound network access only to specified domains. Always `allowlist`.

fields.name: allowed_domains
fields.description: A list of allowed domains when type is `allowlist`.

fields.name: domain_secrets
fields.description: Optional domain-scoped secrets for allowlisted domains.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: The domain associated with the secret.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the secret to inject for the domain.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The secret value to inject for the domain.

types.name: ImageGenTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the image generation tool. Always `image_generation`.

fields.name: quality
fields.description:
The quality of the generated image. One of `low`, `medium`, `high`,
or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

fields.name: size
fields.description: The size of the generated images. For `gpt-image-2` and `gpt-image-2-2026-04-21`, arbitrary resolutions are supported as `WIDTHxHEIGHT` strings, for example `1536x864`. Width and height must both be divisible by 16 and the requested aspect ratio must be between 1:3 and 3:1. Resolutions above `2560x1440` are experimental, and the maximum supported resolution is `3840x2160`. The requested size must also satisfy the model's current pixel and edge limits. The standard sizes `1024x1024`, `1536x1024`, and `1024x1536` are supported by the GPT image models; `auto` is supported for models that allow automatic sizing. For `dall-e-2`, use one of `256x256`, `512x512`, or `1024x1024`. For `dall-e-3`, use one of `1024x1024`, `1792x1024`, or `1024x1792`.

fields.name: output_format
fields.description:
The output format of the generated image. One of `png`, `webp`, or
`jpeg`. Default: `png`.

fields.name: output_compression
fields.description: Compression level for the output image. Default: 100.

fields.name: moderation
fields.description: Moderation level for the generated image. Default: `auto`.

fields.name: background
fields.description:
Background type for the generated image. One of `transparent`,
`opaque`, or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

fields.name: input_image_mask
fields.description:
Optional mask for inpainting. Contains `image_url`
(string, optional) and `file_id` (string, optional).

fields.name: partial_images
fields.description: Number of partial images to generate in streaming mode, from 0 (default value) to 3.

types.description: A tool that generates images using the GPT image models.

types.name: ImageGenToolModelString
types.description: The image generation model to use. Default: `gpt-image-1`.

types.name: ImageGenToolQuality
types.description:
The quality of the generated image. One of `low`, `medium`, `high`,
or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

types.name: ImageGenToolSize
types.description: The size of the generated images. For `gpt-image-2` and `gpt-image-2-2026-04-21`, arbitrary resolutions are supported as `WIDTHxHEIGHT` strings, for example `1536x864`. Width and height must both be divisible by 16 and the requested aspect ratio must be between 1:3 and 3:1. Resolutions above `2560x1440` are experimental, and the maximum supported resolution is `3840x2160`. The requested size must also satisfy the model's current pixel and edge limits. The standard sizes `1024x1024`, `1536x1024`, and `1024x1536` are supported by the GPT image models; `auto` is supported for models that allow automatic sizing. For `dall-e-2`, use one of `256x256`, `512x512`, or `1024x1024`. For `dall-e-3`, use one of `1024x1024`, `1792x1024`, or `1024x1792`.

types.name: ImageGenToolOutputFormat
types.description:
The output format of the generated image. One of `png`, `webp`, or
`jpeg`. Default: `png`.

types.name: ImageGenToolModeration
types.description: Moderation level for the generated image. Default: `auto`.

types.name: ImageGenToolBackground
types.description:
Background type for the generated image. One of `transparent`,
`opaque`, or `auto`. Default: `auto`.

types.name: InputFidelity
types.description: Control how much effort the model will exert to match the style and features, especially facial features, of input images. This parameter is only supported for `gpt-image-1` and `gpt-image-1.5` and later models, unsupported for `gpt-image-1-mini`. Supports `high` and `low`. Defaults to `low`.

types.name: ImageGenToolInputImageMask

fields.name: image_url
fields.description: Base64-encoded mask image.

fields.name: file_id
fields.description: File ID for the mask image.

types.description:
Optional mask for inpainting. Contains `image_url`
(string, optional) and `file_id` (string, optional).

types.name: LocalShellToolParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the local shell tool. Always `local_shell`.

types.description: A tool that allows the model to execute shell commands in a local environment.

types.name: FunctionShellToolParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the shell tool. Always `shell`.

types.description: A tool that allows the model to execute shell commands.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Automatically creates a container for this request

fields.name: file_ids
fields.description: An optional list of uploaded files to make available to your code.

fields.name: network_policy
fields.description: Network access policy for the container.

fields.name: skills
fields.description: An optional list of skills referenced by id or inline data.

fields.name: type
fields.description: References a skill created with the /v1/skills endpoint.

fields.name: skill_id
fields.description: The ID of the referenced skill.

fields.name: version
fields.description: Optional skill version. Use a positive integer or 'latest'. Omit for default.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Defines an inline skill for this request.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the skill.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the skill.

types.name: InlineSkillSourceParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the inline skill source. Must be `base64`.

fields.name: media_type
fields.description: The media type of the inline skill payload. Must be `application/zip`.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Base64-encoded skill zip bundle.

types.description: Inline skill payload

fields.name: type
fields.description: Use a local computer environment.

fields.name: skills
fields.description: An optional list of skills.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the skill.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the skill.

fields.name: path
fields.description: The path to the directory containing the skill.

fields.name: type
fields.description: References a container created with the /v1/containers endpoint

fields.name: container_id
fields.description: The ID of the referenced container.

types.name: CustomToolParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the custom tool. Always `custom`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the custom tool, used to identify it in tool calls.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description of the custom tool, used to provide more context.

fields.name: format
fields.description: The input format for the custom tool. Default is unconstrained text.

fields.name: defer_loading
fields.description: Whether this tool should be deferred and discovered via tool search.

types.description: A custom tool that processes input using a specified format. Learn more about   [custom tools](/docs/guides/function-calling#custom-tools)

types.name: CustomToolParamFormat
types.description: The input format for the custom tool. Default is unconstrained text.

types.name: CustomTextFormatParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Unconstrained text format. Always `text`.

types.description: Unconstrained free-form text.

types.name: CustomGrammarFormatParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Grammar format. Always `grammar`.

fields.name: definition
fields.description: The grammar definition.

types.description: A grammar defined by the user.

types.name: NamespaceToolParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool. Always `namespace`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The namespace name used in tool calls (for example, `crm`).

fields.name: description
fields.description: A description of the namespace shown to the model.

fields.name: tools
fields.description: The function/custom tools available inside this namespace.

types.description: Groups function/custom tools under a shared namespace.

types.name: NamespaceToolParamToolsItem
types.description: A function or custom tool that belongs to a namespace.

fields.name: defer_loading
fields.description: Whether this function should be deferred and discovered via tool search.

types.name: ToolSearchToolParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool. Always `tool_search`.

types.description: Hosted or BYOT tool search configuration for deferred tools.

types.name: WebSearchPreviewTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the web search tool. One of `web_search_preview` or `web_search_preview_2025_03_11`.

types.description: This tool searches the web for relevant results to use in a response. Learn more about the [web search tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-web-search).

types.name: WebSearchPreviewToolType
types.description: The type of the web search tool. One of `web_search_preview` or `web_search_preview_2025_03_11`.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of location approximation. Always `approximate`.

types.name: ApplyPatchToolParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool. Always `apply_patch`.

types.description: Allows the assistant to create, delete, or update files using unified diffs.

types.name: ToolChoiceParam
types.description:
How the model should select which tool (or tools) to use when generating
a response. See the `tools` parameter to see how to specify which tools
the model can call.

types.name: ToolChoiceOptions
types.description:
Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.

`none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message.

`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or
more tools.

`required` means the model must call one or more tools.

types.name: ToolChoiceAllowed

fields.name: type
fields.description: Allowed tool configuration type. Always `allowed_tools`.

fields.name: mode
fields.description:
Constrains the tools available to the model to a pre-defined set.

`auto` allows the model to pick from among the allowed tools and generate a
message.

`required` requires the model to call one or more of the allowed tools.

fields.name: tools
fields.description:
A list of tool definitions that the model should be allowed to call.

For the Responses API, the list of tool definitions might look like:
```json
[
  { "type": "function", "name": "get_weather" },
  { "type": "mcp", "server_label": "deepwiki" },
  { "type": "image_generation" }
]
```

types.description: Constrains the tools available to the model to a pre-defined set.

types.name: ToolChoiceAllowedMode
types.description:
Constrains the tools available to the model to a pre-defined set.

`auto` allows the model to pick from among the allowed tools and generate a
message.

`required` requires the model to call one or more of the allowed tools.

types.name: ToolChoiceTypes

fields.name: type
fields.description:
The type of hosted tool the model should to use. Learn more about
[built-in tools](/docs/guides/tools).

Allowed values are:
- `file_search`
- `web_search_preview`
- `computer`
- `computer_use_preview`
- `computer_use`
- `code_interpreter`
- `image_generation`

types.description:
Indicates that the model should use a built-in tool to generate a response.
[Learn more about built-in tools](/docs/guides/tools).

types.name: ToolChoiceTypesType
types.description:
The type of hosted tool the model should to use. Learn more about
[built-in tools](/docs/guides/tools).

Allowed values are:
- `file_search`
- `web_search_preview`
- `computer`
- `computer_use_preview`
- `computer_use`
- `code_interpreter`
- `image_generation`

types.name: ToolChoiceFunction

fields.name: type
fields.description: For function calling, the type is always `function`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to call.

types.description: Use this option to force the model to call a specific function.

types.name: ToolChoiceMcp

fields.name: type
fields.description: For MCP tools, the type is always `mcp`.

fields.name: server_label
fields.description: The label of the MCP server to use.

types.description: Use this option to force the model to call a specific tool on a remote MCP server.

types.name: ToolChoiceCustom

fields.name: type
fields.description: For custom tool calling, the type is always `custom`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the custom tool to call.

types.description: Use this option to force the model to call a specific custom tool.

types.name: SpecificApplyPatchParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The tool to call. Always `apply_patch`.

types.description: Forces the model to call the apply_patch tool when executing a tool call.

types.name: SpecificFunctionShellParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: The tool to call. Always `shell`.

types.description: Forces the model to call the shell tool when a tool call is required.

types.name: Prompt

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the prompt template to use.

types.description:
Reference to a prompt template and its variables.
[Learn more](/docs/guides/text?api-mode=responses#reusable-prompts).

types.name: InputTextContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_text`.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text input to the model.

types.description: A text input to the model.

types.name: InputImageContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_image`.

types.description: An image input to the model. Learn about [image inputs](/docs/guides/vision).

types.name: InputFileContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_file`.

fields.name: filename
fields.description: The name of the file to be sent to the model.

fields.name: file_data
fields.description: The content of the file to be sent to the model.

fields.name: file_url
fields.description: The URL of the file to be sent to the model.

types.description: A file input to the model.

types.name: ResponseTruncation
types.description:
The truncation strategy to use for the model response.
- `auto`: If the input to this Response exceeds
  the model's context window size, the model will truncate the
  response to fit the context window by dropping items from the beginning of the conversation.
- `disabled` (default): If the input size will exceed the context window
  size for a model, the request will fail with a 400 error.

types.name: ResponseStatus
types.description:
The status of the response generation. One of `completed`, `failed`,
`in_progress`, `cancelled`, `queued`, or `incomplete`.

types.name: ResponseError

fields.name: message
fields.description: A human-readable description of the error.

types.description: An error object returned when the model fails to generate a Response.

types.name: ResponseErrorCode
types.description: The error code for the response.

types.name: ResponseIncompleteDetails

fields.name: reason
fields.description: The reason why the response is incomplete.

types.description: Details about why the response is incomplete.

types.name: ResponseIncompleteDetailsReason
types.description: The reason why the response is incomplete.

types.name: OutputMessage

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique ID of the output message.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the output message. Always `message`.

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the output message. Always `assistant`.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The content of the output message.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
The status of the message input. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when input items are returned via API.

types.description: An output message from the model.

types.name: OutputTextContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the output text. Always `output_text`.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text output from the model.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: The annotations of the text output.

types.description: A text output from the model.

types.name: Annotation
types.description: An annotation that applies to a span of output text.

types.name: FileCitationBody

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the file citation. Always `file_citation`.

fields.name: file_id
fields.description: The ID of the file.

fields.name: index
fields.description: The index of the file in the list of files.

fields.name: filename
fields.description: The filename of the file cited.

types.description: A citation to a file.

types.name: UrlCitationBody

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the URL citation. Always `url_citation`.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL of the web resource.

fields.name: start_index
fields.description: The index of the first character of the URL citation in the message.

fields.name: end_index
fields.description: The index of the last character of the URL citation in the message.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The title of the web resource.

types.description: A citation for a web resource used to generate a model response.

types.name: ContainerFileCitationBody

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the container file citation. Always `container_file_citation`.

fields.name: container_id
fields.description: The ID of the container file.

fields.name: file_id
fields.description: The ID of the file.

fields.name: start_index
fields.description: The index of the first character of the container file citation in the message.

fields.name: end_index
fields.description: The index of the last character of the container file citation in the message.

fields.name: filename
fields.description: The filename of the container file cited.

types.description: A citation for a container file used to generate a model response.

types.name: FilePath

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the file path. Always `file_path`.

fields.name: file_id
fields.description: The ID of the file.

fields.name: index
fields.description: The index of the file in the list of files.

types.description: A path to a file.

types.name: LogProb
types.description: The log probability of a token.

types.name: TopLogProb
types.description: The top log probability of a token.

types.name: RefusalContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the refusal. Always `refusal`.

fields.name: refusal
fields.description: The refusal explanation from the model.

types.description: A refusal from the model.

types.name: MessagePhase
types.description:
Labels an `assistant` message as intermediate commentary (`commentary`) or the final answer (`final_answer`).
For models like `gpt-5.3-codex` and beyond, when sending follow-up requests, preserve and resend
phase on all assistant messages — dropping it can degrade performance. Not used for user messages.

types.name: OutputMessageStatus
types.description:
The status of the message input. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when input items are returned via API.

types.name: FileSearchToolCall

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique ID of the file search tool call.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the file search tool call. Always `file_search_call`.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
The status of the file search tool call. One of `in_progress`,
`searching`, `incomplete` or `failed`,

fields.name: queries
fields.description: The queries used to search for files.

types.description:
The results of a file search tool call. See the
[file search guide](/docs/guides/tools-file-search) for more information.

types.name: FileSearchToolCallStatus
types.description:
The status of the file search tool call. One of `in_progress`,
`searching`, `incomplete` or `failed`,

fields.name: file_id
fields.description: The unique ID of the file.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text that was retrieved from the file.

fields.name: filename
fields.description: The name of the file.

fields.name: score
fields.description: The relevance score of the file - a value between 0 and 1.

types.name: FunctionToolCall

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique ID of the function tool call.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the function tool call. Always `function_call`.

fields.name: call_id
fields.description: The unique ID of the function tool call generated by the model.

fields.name: namespace
fields.description: The namespace of the function to run.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to run.

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: A JSON string of the arguments to pass to the function.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

types.description:
A tool call to run a function. See the 
[function calling guide](/docs/guides/function-calling) for more information.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique ID of the function call tool output.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the function tool call output. Always `function_call_output`.

fields.name: call_id
fields.description: The unique ID of the function tool call generated by the model.

fields.name: output
fields.description:
The output from the function call generated by your code.
Can be a string or an list of output content.

fields.name: created_by
fields.description: The identifier of the actor that created the item.

types.name: FunctionToolCallOutputResourceOutput
types.description:
The output from the function call generated by your code.
Can be a string or an list of output content.

types.name: WebSearchToolCall

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique ID of the web search tool call.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the web search tool call. Always `web_search_call`.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The status of the web search tool call.

fields.name: action
fields.description:
An object describing the specific action taken in this web search call.
Includes details on how the model used the web (search, open_page, find_in_page).

types.description:
The results of a web search tool call. See the
[web search guide](/docs/guides/tools-web-search) for more information.

types.name: WebSearchToolCallStatus
types.description: The status of the web search tool call.

types.name: WebSearchToolCallAction
types.description:
An object describing the specific action taken in this web search call.
Includes details on how the model used the web (search, open_page, find_in_page).

fields.name: type
fields.description: The action type.

fields.name: query
fields.description: [DEPRECATED] The search query.

fields.name: queries
fields.description: The search queries.

fields.name: sources
fields.description: The sources used in the search.

types.description: Action type "search" - Performs a web search query.

types.name: ComputerToolCall

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the computer call. Always `computer_call`.

fields.name: call_id
fields.description: An identifier used when responding to the tool call with output.

The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

types.description:
A tool call to a computer use tool. See the
[computer use guide](/docs/guides/tools-computer-use) for more information.

types.name: ClickParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Specifies the event type. For a click action, this property is always `click`.

fields.name: x
fields.description: The x-coordinate where the click occurred.

fields.name: y
fields.description: The y-coordinate where the click occurred.

types.description: A click action.

types.name: DoubleClickAction

fields.name: type
fields.description: Specifies the event type. For a double click action, this property is always set to `double_click`.

fields.name: x
fields.description: The x-coordinate where the double click occurred.

fields.name: y
fields.description: The y-coordinate where the double click occurred.

types.description: A double click action.

types.name: DragParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Specifies the event type. For a drag action, this property is always set to `drag`.

fields.name: path
fields.description:
An array of coordinates representing the path of the drag action. Coordinates will appear as an array of objects, eg
```
[
  { x: 100, y: 200 },
  { x: 200, y: 300 }
]
```

types.description: A drag action.

types.name: CoordParam

fields.name: x
fields.description: The x-coordinate.

fields.name: y
fields.description: The y-coordinate.

types.description: An x/y coordinate pair, e.g. `{ x: 100, y: 200 }`.

types.name: KeyPressAction

fields.name: type
fields.description: Specifies the event type. For a keypress action, this property is always set to `keypress`.

fields.name: keys
fields.description: The combination of keys the model is requesting to be pressed. This is an array of strings, each representing a key.

types.description: A collection of keypresses the model would like to perform.

types.name: MoveParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Specifies the event type. For a move action, this property is always set to `move`.

fields.name: x
fields.description: The x-coordinate to move to.

fields.name: y
fields.description: The y-coordinate to move to.

types.description: A mouse move action.

types.name: ScreenshotParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Specifies the event type. For a screenshot action, this property is always set to `screenshot`.

types.description: A screenshot action.

types.name: ScrollParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Specifies the event type. For a scroll action, this property is always set to `scroll`.

fields.name: x
fields.description: The x-coordinate where the scroll occurred.

fields.name: y
fields.description: The y-coordinate where the scroll occurred.

fields.name: scroll_x
fields.description: The horizontal scroll distance.

fields.name: scroll_y
fields.description: The vertical scroll distance.

types.description: A scroll action.

types.name: TypeParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Specifies the event type. For a type action, this property is always set to `type`.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text to type.

types.description: An action to type in text.

types.name: WaitParam

fields.name: type
fields.description: Specifies the event type. For a wait action, this property is always set to `wait`.

types.description: A wait action.

types.name: ReasoningItem

fields.description: The type of the object. Always `reasoning`.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the reasoning content.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: Reasoning summary content.

The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

types.description:
A description of the chain of thought used by a reasoning model while generating
a response. Be sure to include these items in your `input` to the Responses API
for subsequent turns of a conversation if you are manually
[managing context](/docs/guides/conversation-state).

The output from the custom tool call generated by your code.
Can be a string or an list of output content.

fields.name: created_by

types.name: ResponseInstructions
types.description:
A system (or developer) message inserted into the model's context.

When using along with `previous_response_id`, the instructions from a previous
response will not be carried over to the next response. This makes it simple
to swap out system (or developer) messages in new responses.

types.name: EasyInputMessage

fields.name: role
fields.description:
The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or
`developer`.

fields.name: content
fields.description:
Text, image, or audio input to the model, used to generate a response.
Can also contain previous assistant responses.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the message input. Always `message`.

types.description:
A message input to the model with a role indicating instruction following
hierarchy. Instructions given with the `developer` or `system` role take
precedence over instructions given with the `user` role. Messages with the
`assistant` role are presumed to have been generated by the model in previous
interactions.

types.name: EasyInputMessageRole
types.description:
The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or
`developer`.

types.name: EasyInputMessageContent
types.description:
Text, image, or audio input to the model, used to generate a response.
Can also contain previous assistant responses.

types.name: Item
types.description: Content item used to generate a response.

types.name: InputMessage

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the message input. Always set to `message`.

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the message input. One of `user`, `system`, or `developer`.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
The status of item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

types.description:
A message input to the model with a role indicating instruction following
hierarchy. Instructions given with the `developer` or `system` role take
precedence over instructions given with the `user` role.

types.name: InputMessageRole
types.description: The role of the message input. One of `user`, `system`, or `developer`.

types.name: Conversation2

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique ID of the conversation that this response was associated with.

types.description: The conversation that this response belonged to. Input items and output items from this response were automatically added to this conversation.

methods.description: Retrieves a model response with the given ID.

pathParams.name: response_id
pathParams.description: The ID of the response to retrieve.

queryParams.name: include
queryParams.description:
Additional fields to include in the response. See the `include`
parameter for Response creation above for more information.

queryParams.name: stream
queryParams.description:
If set to true, the model response data will be streamed to the client
as it is generated using [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#Event_stream_format).
See the [Streaming section below](/docs/api-reference/responses-streaming)
for more information.

queryParams.name: starting_after
queryParams.description: The sequence number of the event after which to start streaming.

queryParams.name: include_obfuscation
queryParams.description:
When true, stream obfuscation will be enabled. Stream obfuscation adds
random characters to an `obfuscation` field on streaming delta events
to normalize payload sizes as a mitigation to certain side-channel
attacks. These obfuscation fields are included by default, but add a
small amount of overhead to the data stream. You can set
`include_obfuscation` to false to optimize for bandwidth if you trust
the network links between your application and the OpenAI API.
